# function: cidr_wildcard

Returns the wildcard mask for the given IPv4 CIDR. For example, `10.0.0.0/24` → `0.0.0.255`. IPv6 CIDRs return an error.

**Common uses:** generating Cisco IOS ACL entries, AWS network ACL wildcard fields, firewall rules that use wildcard mask notation instead of prefix length.

## Example Usage

```terraform
// Cisco-style wildcard mask (bitwise inverse of the subnet mask). IPv4 only.
output "acl_mask" {
  value = provider::burnham::cidr_wildcard("10.0.0.0/24")
  // → "0.0.0.255"
}
```

## Signature

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
cidr_wildcard(cidr string) string
```

## Arguments

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `cidr` (String) An IPv4 CIDR.
